title: rosa viscaria var. normalis
intro: Rosa viscaria var. normalis, also known as Alpine Rose or Alpine Rosehip, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family and is native to the alpine regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its high content of vitamins A and C, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a popular natural remedy for various health conditions. Rosa viscaria var. normalis can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or added to food products to harness its medicinal properties.
